title: CAG flags ₹3,541 crore excess spend, budgetary lapses in Maharashtra Govt’s Ladki Bahin scheme
content: The CAG said audit scrutiny revealed that ₹15,586 crore drawn between January and March 2025 was transferred to Virtual Personal Deposit Accounts (VPDAs)

title: Supreme Court to hear plea against sky-high airfares today
content: The petition said the government’s answer that “new rules are being framed, trust the process”  was just not enough

title: SIR anomalies may cross one crore in Telangana: CEO
content: In an exclusive interview with The Hindu, Chief Electoral Officer C. Sudharshan Reddy provides more insights into the ongoing SIR process

title: ‘We tried CPR, but it was too late,’ say Vietnam boat tragedy witnesses on return to Hyderabad
content: Several Indian tourists who were stranded in Vietnam after the tragedy landed in Hyderabad on the night of July 12

title: Bihar CM Samrat Choudhary inaugurates projects worth ₹1,047 crore
content: Mr. Choudhary said that Bihar’s economy was being significantly strengthened by promoting women’s economic empowerment and entrepreneurship.

title: West Bengal government seeks support of DMSC for cervical cancer vaccination for sex workers in Sonagachi
content: Durbar Mahila Samanwaya Committee, a collective for working on rights and recognition of sex workers, celebrates 31 years

title: Prospects of kharif cultivation turns bleak in Telangana with monsoon failure
content: Over 63% of mandals in 32 rural districts record deficient to large deficient rainfall; water levels in reservoirs remains gloomy

title: Road rage | Politics of renaming a street in Bengal
content: Soon after the BJP government renamed Kolkata’s Suhrawardy Avenue as Gopal Mukherjee Road, there was public outcry — and historical evidence — that the street was originally named not after the man whom the government blames for killings, but rather the first Muslim V-C of Calcutta University. Here’s why the renaming of the street marks a crucial moment in Bengal politics

title: Hyderabad SI suspended after letting six-year-old granddaughter drive, claims he was preparing her for Guinness World Record
content: Video shows the child behind the wheel on the busy Gandhamguda–Bairagiguda road as traffic piles up behind the car; the vehicle already had six pending traffic challans worth ₹3,800

title: ISRO successfully carries out tests of Gaganyaan crew module systems
content: One of the tests involved separation of the ‘umbilical mechanism’ that serves as a link between the crew module, where astronauts live, and the service module that provides propulsion, says the agency

title: Mehbooba placed under ‘house arrest’ ahead of Martyrs’ Day; PDP raises rights concerns
content: Says democratic society cannot flourish where people continue to live under an environment of constant apprehension

title: Singer Janaki cremated with state honours in Mysuru
content: Janaki’s granddaughter Apsara Vydyula performed the last rites as per Andhra Brahmin traditions in the presence of her close relatives, people’s representatives, friends, and a large number of admirers and the general public

title: Doctor who flagged equipment shortage lauds Kerala government’s move to end floor treatment
content: Haris Chirakkal, Head of Urology department at Government Medical College Hospital, Thiruvananthapuram, had hit the headlines after flagging shortages of essential surgical equipment and delays in surgeries during the previous LDF government’s tenure

title: AAP launches signature drive to press for action against ‘dacoity’ at Ram Temple
content: Kejriwal urges devotees across the country to organise Hanuman Chalisa recitals and collect signatures

title: Ernakulam to get government-run geriatric centre
content: The district panchayat has initiated steps to establish a geriatric centre which will include an inpatient physiotherapy centre and a dialysis centre

title: Palakkad-Kozhikode highway construction to get under way soon
content: Six-lane highway under the Union government’s Bharatmala project is back on track, with over 95% of land acquisition completed

title: Operation Toofan: Kozhikode City police arrest 58 in single-day sweep
content: 57 narcotics cases were registered during flash searches carried out at various locations under the jurisdiction of the Kozhikode City police

title: Teacher suspended for ‘beating’ student
content: The school management suspended the teacher after he was arrested following a complaint lodged by the child’s parents

title: Barricades come up in Srinagar on eve of 1931 revolt anniversary
content: Since the Centre ended J&K’s special status in 2019, the L-G administration disallows regional parties from paying tributes at the Naqshband Sahib shrine

title: One Indian seafarer missing in attack on container ship; govt. issues strong condemnation
content: Ship’s third engineer missing and his family has been informed; families of rescued Indian seafarers have been informed and counselled; “targeting of commercial shipping and civilian infrastructure in the region must end,” says govt.

title: Sainik School hosts motivational talk on globe circumnavigation and Everest expedition
content: Lieutenant Commanders of the Indian Navy, Dilna K. and Roopa A., who circumnavigated the globe aboard the Indian Naval Sailing Vessel (INSV) Tarini, and Zuhra Cherukappalli, an accomplished mountaineer who scaled Mount Everest, led the motivational lecture and interactive session ‘Talk on Circumnavigation of the Globe and Mount Everest Expedition’

title: Accept party’s bypoll choice, says Narottam Mishra after Delhi visit
content: BJP leader Narottam Mishra went to Delhi where, sources said, he met party president Nitin Nabin; though Mr. Mishra said he went there “just like that”, sources said he was told to rein in his supporters

title: Andhra Pradesh Education Department launches ‘Mission March SSC-2027’ for 100% pass rate
content: As part of the initiative, schools will conduct a dedicated academic support session every working day from 4 p.m. to 5 p.m., where teachers will focus on subject revision, competency-based practice, and daily slip tests to assess students’ learning progress and identify areas requiring improvement

title: Venkaiah Naidu urges youth to embrace ideology-based politics
content: Former Vice-President says party-hopping should follow resignation from elected posts, calls for a national debate on anti-defection law, and urges political leaders to uphold civility in public life

title: India discusses agricultural collaboration with Taliban government of Afghanistan
content: Two sides review ongoing cooperation and explore new avenues for collaboration in agriculture, irrigation, livestock, agricultural research, education, capacity building and agri-trade among other things

title: Assam CM announces ‘ruthless’ anti-drugs drive
content: Launching a State-level drugs disposal campaign, Himanta Biswa Sarma says inter-State coordination has improved after the installation of a BJP government in West Bengal

title: Water Authority steps up pace of Jal Jeevan Mission work in Thiruvananthapuram district
content: Several of the water supply schemes and components necessary for providing connectivity in the rural areas of the district are expected to be commissioned over this year and the next

title: Vietnam boat tragedy: families lose sole breadwinners in Vellore, Tiruvannamalai
content: C. Vinaya Kumar, 45, and S. Ravisankar, 51, had both gone to Vietnam as part of a trip organised Lava Mobiles

title: Tiruchi residents among those killed in Vietnam boat accident
content: N. Balaji, A.M. Sheikh Abdullah, and S. Alagurajan, were residents of Tiruchi who were killed in the Vietnam boat accident

title: West Bengal BJP chief calls AIFB members goons; Forward Bloc demands apology
content: Bengal BJP chief Samik Bhattacharya says when Syama Prasad Mookerjee urged Subhas Chandra Bose and his brother Sarat Chandra Bose to raise their voice against torture of Hindus in Bangladesh, none of them listened

title: Assam activist leading protests against Kaziranga luxury hotels detained
content: Guwahati police say Pranab Doley, booked under multiple sections of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, will be handed over to their Bokakhat counterparts in eastern Assam

title: Villagers oppose establishment of sewage treatment plant near Arcot
content: Residents of Pindithangal village in Ranipet district have been up against the establishment of a sewage treatment plant by the neighbouring Kalavai town panchayat in their village. They are worried that the groundwater table will be contaminated in Pindithangal and the nearby farming villages, where farmers depend on borewells and waterbodies for irrigation

title: Tamil Nadu will witness change in 2031 similar to 2026, says Annamalai
content: Annamalai says his organisation will not resort to indiscriminate criticism of the government or individuals, highlights that the organisation has enrolled over 19 lakh members and expresses confidence in reaching 50 lakh members, after which the organisation can establish itself as a political party for social good

title: 'Smoke was everywhere': How Bangkok's pub inferno killed 27 - 10 points
content: Tragedy struck at Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao pub, where a fierce fire resulted in the deaths of twenty-seven people. Victims found themselves ensnared in the pub's bathrooms as they tried to escape. Smoke filled the venue quickly, contributing to the catastrophe. Emergency responders faced significant hurdles when attempting to rescue those inside. Authorities have launched an investigation to uncover what led to this heartbreaking event.

title: Rohit Sharma undertakes match-simulation in nets ahead of ODI series opener
content: Rohit Sharma put in an extensive match-simulation batting session during India's training at Edgbaston ahead of the first ODI against England, while Virat Kohli sharpened his game against both pace and spin before sharing valuable insights with young pacer Gurnoor Brar as preparations gathered momentum.

title: 'Will our home still be standing tomorrow?' The Northeast relives its annual flood heartbreak
content: Northeast India faces severe monsoon floods and landslides, affecting nearly 50,000 people in Assam. Rising waters have submerged villages and damaged agricultural land, impacting livelihoods. Across the region, homes and infrastructure have suffered extensive damage from heavy rainfall. Thousands have been displaced and are seeking refuge in the relief camps.

title: Jeff Bezos' ex-wife MacKenzie Scott donates to US youth mental health non-profit
content: MacKenzie Scott has donated twenty million dollars to Active Minds. This significant contribution is the largest in the nonprofit's history. Active Minds focuses on youth mental health awareness and reducing stigma. The unrestricted funds will expand programs and support young leaders. This donation occurs amid growing concerns about youth mental health.

title: 'Jaldi jaldi me jo mila ... ': Sooryavanshi reveals Abhishek arranged his Wimbledon outfit
content: Teenage Indian cricketer Vaibhav Sooryavanshi attended the Wimbledon men's singles final. He watched Jannik Sinner win his second Wimbledon title against Alexander Zverev. Sooryavanshi, who recently debuted for India, was dressed formally for the occasion. He admitted his outfit was arranged quickly in the last minute. Several other Indian cricketers were also present at the prestigious event.

title: Techie found dead on railway tracks after slitting colleague's throat in Gurgaon
content: A software engineer allegedly killed his colleague before dying by suicide on railway tracks. Police discovered the woman's body with her throat slit in the man's accommodation. The man's body was found on the tracks, initially treated as an accident. Relatives identified the man after his family was informed about his death. Investigations are ongoing to establish the motive and sequence of events.

title: DU UG 2026 simulated rank list released for over 2.06 lakh candidates
content: The University of Delhi has published the DU UG 2026 simulated rank list for over 2.06 lakh candidates on its undergraduate admission portal. The tentative ranks have been prepared using CUET 2026 scores and submitted preferences. Candidates can revise their choices before the final ranks are determined. The university has also outlined tie-breaking rules, excluded performance-based courses from simulated rankings, and confirmed that the first CSAS 2026 seat allotment will be announced on July 16.

title: Ousted Twitter CEO Parag Agrawal says Microsoft's Satya Nadella inspired him to become a founder
content: Parag Agrawal found inspiration in Satya Nadella's leadership for Twitter's transformation. He viewed Nadella as a re-founder who completely changed a business and culture. Agrawal planned radical internal projects like Project Saturn and Birdwatch for Twitter. These initiatives aimed to significantly alter user experience and content moderation. His tenure ended abruptly, preventing the full realization of these ambitious plans.

title: Chaos returns to Middle East: Oil prices jump 4% as US-Iran exchange strikes
content: Oil prices surged sharply as renewed military action between Iran and the United States escalated tensions.  Fresh strikes expanded to Qatar and the United Arab Emirates over the weekend.  This escalation has clouded the future of the interim US-Iran agreement signed last month.  Market participants remain cautious about how the developing situation could unfold.  Iran's Ministry of Foreign Affairs issued a strong condemnation of the latest US military strikes.

title: Baldwins pull Hamptons home after cutting price from $29M to $18.99M
content: Alec and Hilaria Baldwin have again removed their Hamptons home from the market. The property was recently relisted at a reduced price of $18.99 million.  This marks several attempts over the past few years to sell the Amagansett residence.  The family cited their children's attachment and plans for a new adventure.  They will now divide summers between Spain and their Hamptons property.

title: Jeff Bezos wrote Amazon’s plan while MacKenzie Scott drove across US
content: While on a 2,000-mile road trip, Jeff Bezos envisioned the business plan for Amazon, ultimately leaving a high-paying Wall Street career to turn that vision into reality. With his wife, MacKenzie Scott, handling essential operations, they started small, predicting moderate sales. However, the response surpassed their expectations, leading Amazon to evolve well beyond its original online bookstore model.

title: Apple lawsuit reveals what ex-iPhone engineer allegedly took before joining OpenAI
content: Apple has filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of stealing confidential trade secrets. The company alleges a former engineer took sensitive files and knowledge after leaving. OpenAI allegedly encouraged job candidates to share Apple's proprietary information. This information was reportedly used to accelerate OpenAI's own hardware projects. OpenAI denies any interest in acquiring other companies' trade secrets.

title: Gulf war: US steps up strikes to 'degrade' Iran's ability to attack ships, mariners at Hormuz
content: United States forces conducted new strikes against Iran early Monday morning. These operations aimed to degrade Iran's capability to attack commercial shipping vessels. Iran reported explosions in southern provinces and condemned the United States' actions. The Strait of Hormuz remains a central point of ongoing conflict and tension. Diplomatic efforts between the two nations have reportedly collapsed following recent incidents.

title: Who are Jannik Sinner’s parents? Meet dad Johann and mom Siglinde
content: Jannik Sinner, the world No. 1 tennis player and four-time Grand Slam champion, credits much of his drive to his parents, Johann and Siglinde Sinner. The couple worked for years at a ski lodge in the Italian Dolomites before the family established their own guest house. Johann occasionally joins Sinner on tour as a personal chef, while Siglinde is known to struggle watching her son's matches live due to nerves. Both were present at Wimbledon 2025 when Sinner defended his title.

title: NHL trade rumours: Utah Mammoth, Sharks eye $59M Winnipeg Jets goalie
content: The Winnipeg Jets’ star goaltender Connor Hellebuyck has been linked to the Buffalo Sabres heavily in the last few weeks before the deal reportedly fell through, a few days ago. While he has since been speculated to join a number of rival teams, nothing has been confirmed yet. Amid this, the Utah Mammoth and the San Jose Sharks have emerged as top landing spots.

title: Who are Jude Bellingham's parents? Meet Denise and Mark Bellingham
content: Denise and Mark Bellingham have been central to Jude Bellingham’s rise from a young footballer in Birmingham to one of England’s biggest stars. Through career sacrifices, constant support, and important decisions, they have helped shape both Jude and Jobe’s careers. While reports have questioned their relationship, they continue to stand behind their sons’ success.

title: Why Zverev told Wimbledon champion Sinner, 'I don't really like you anymore'
content: Jannik Sinner secured his second consecutive Wimbledon title after defeating Alexander Zverev in the final. Zverev humorously acknowledged his losing streak against Sinner during the trophy ceremony. Sinner's victory marked his fifth Grand Slam title and a strong comeback performance. Despite his loss, Zverev will ascend to world number two in the rankings. Carlos Alcaraz dropped in rankings due to injury absence from tournaments.

title: Vietnam tragedy: Day after death of 15 Indians, speedboat captain held
content: Vietnamese police arrested the speedboat captain after the vessel capsized near Phu Quoc island. Fifteen Indian tourists tragically died when the boat overturned in rough seas on Saturday. Rescuers recovered twenty-one individuals from the water, and authorities are investigating the incident. The remains of the deceased Indians have reached Ho Chi Minh City for repatriation. Most rescued Indian tourists are returning home, while one remains hospitalized.

title: Bangkok pub turns death trap as massive overnight fire kills at least 27
content: A massive fire ripped through a Bangkok pub early Monday, killing at least twenty-seven people. Several others were injured and taken to the hospital for treatment. The blaze broke out around midnight at an entertainment venue in the northern part of the city. Firefighters battled the flames for about half an hour to bring the blaze under control. Authorities have launched an investigation into the cause of the deadly fire.

title: Mexico highway crash kills 9, injures 10 after truck slams into emergency response vehicles
content: Nine people, including two children, died after a truck crashed into earlier accident responders. Ten others sustained injuries, with two National Guard officers seriously hurt. Four United States citizens suffered minor injuries in the devastating highway collision. Emergency crews were attending a prior accident involving two trucks when this occurred. Investigations are currently underway to determine the cause of the second crash.

title: Foreign investors return to India, equities lead revival
content: Foreign investors have returned to Indian markets, showing renewed interest in equities during July. This shift follows months of consistent selling, with significant inflows recorded in early July. Equities have become the primary destination for foreign capital, attracting substantial investments. Debt markets also continue to see inflows, extending momentum from previous months. Stronger economic fundamentals and a stable currency are contributing to this positive investment trend.

title: At 18, Maya Merhige completes 21.4-mile North Channel swim in 12 hrs, nears world record
content: Eighteen-year-old open-water swimmer Maya Merhige has moved one step closer to making history after successfully completing the 21.4-mile North Channel swim from Ireland to Scotland. If she completes the remaining two swims on schedule, she is expected to become the youngest person to complete the Oceans Seven, earning a Guinness World Record.

title: Jannik Sinner wins second straight Wimbledon title, beats Alexander Zverev in final
content: Jannik Sinner secured his second Wimbledon championship on Sunday. He defeated Alexander Zverev in a hard-fought four-set match. Zverev sustained an injury during the third set, impacting his performance. Sinner's victory marked his fifth Grand Slam title overall. The win also extended Sinner's dominance over Zverev to ten consecutive matches.

title: 'We want you tomorrow': Bangladesh official slams Sheikh Hasina's delayed return
content: Bangladesh's International Crimes Tribunal prosecutor wants Sheikh Hasina to surrender immediately. She faces a death sentence and jail upon returning from India. The prosecutor stated Hasina would be sent directly to jail without court appearance. He warned against any stunts or creating further instability in the country. The tribunal's activities have gained momentum under the current government.

title: After brief burst of rain, monsoon in dry phase, deficit rises to 18%
content: India's monsoon has weakened again after a week of good showers. The nationwide rainfall deficit has widened to eighteen percent on Sunday. Subdued rainfall activity is predicted over several key regions for the next week. This dry spell could undermine recent gains in agricultural sowing activities. Fifteen states are currently reporting significant rainfall deficits of twenty percent or more.

title: CM Omar Adullah to BJP: Should I ask Trump for J&K statehood?
content: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah expressed frustration over the delayed restoration of statehood. He questioned the central government's stance on protests and assembly discussions regarding this issue. Abdullah announced a protest at Jantar Mantar in New Delhi on July 20. He reminded Prime Minister Narendra Modi of his past assurance on the matter. The National Conference will continue its democratic campaign until statehood is reinstated.

title: Dhar's Saraswati is Gayatri: Science ends 900-year mix-up
content: A 12th-century sculpture previously identified as Saraswati has been correctly identified as Gayatri. Digital documentation and 3D mapping revealed iconographic clues missed for centuries. The absence of a veena, a common Saraswati attribute, proved crucial in this re-identification. This rare image of Gayatri, embodying Vedic wisdom, was unearthed in Dhar. The rediscovered icon now represents Madhya Pradesh's digital heritage mission.

title: With connectivity gains, government schools narrow digital divide
content: Government schools have significantly improved internet connectivity and smart classroom access. Their internet coverage increased substantially, narrowing the gap with private institutions. Computer availability also saw improvements across all school management types. Smart classroom coverage nationally rose, with government schools showing notable gains. Digital libraries remain scarce across most educational institutions.

title: Why apex court's wading into Darbhanga's ponds
content: The Supreme Court has agreed to hear a case concerning alleged encroachment of three historic water bodies. Petitioners accuse the Bihar government of threatening these lakes through a beautification project. Locals argue the government's plan constitutes encroachment disguised as development. The case highlights a broader decline in Bihar's water bodies over decades. Experts emphasize the need for ecosystem preservation and community involvement.

title: ‘Alpha’ box office collection day 10: Film nears Rs 90 crore worldwide
content: Alpha's second weekend at the box office showcased impressive growth, securing its position as a strong contender. With Alia Bhatt and Sharvari leading the cast, the film has almost reached the remarkable Rs 90 crore mark globally by Sunday. In India alone, it has grossed Rs 63.91 crore over ten days, complemented by Rs 25.50 crore from international markets, resulting in a net domestic earning of Rs 53.60 crore.

title: Ayodhya Ram temple donation row: SIT flags near-total breakdown of safeguards
content: A SIT probe found nine critical lapses in donation handling procedures. These security and procedural failures allowed for alleged large-scale embezzlement of funds. Counting personnel were not frisked and pocketless uniforms were not enforced. Cash from various boxes was mixed, and no denomination inventory was prepared. Biometric attendance was also not effectively implemented, undermining accountability.

title: Vietnam boat tragedy eyewitnesses claim crew negligence, treatment delays
content: Eyewitnesses blamed crew negligence and delayed medical care for the Vietnam boat disaster. Rough seas and gusty winds caused the speedboat to overturn within seconds. Many tourists drowned while trapped beneath the capsized vessel despite wearing life jackets. Rescue boats arrived nearly three hours later, and emergency treatment was delayed. Authorities have not yet responded to the allegations made by survivors.

title: Liquor gangs go to war in 'dry' Bihar, 3 dead in 4 days
content: Three alleged liquor smugglers died in Samastipur district over four days. One incident involved the murder of Prabhat Choudhary and his associate Sunny Kumar. Another victim, Ishant Kumar, was kidnapped and murdered, his body later found buried. Police suspect financial disputes and prior working relationships led to these killings. Authorities are intensifying their crackdown on liquor smuggling networks in the region.

title: Food security calls at risk? Stats ministry flags serious gaps in agri data collection
content: Official crop enumeration inspections were completed on time in less than half of the country's villages. Many village maps used for land tracing are over twenty years old and outdated. Crop-cutting experiments are being conducted by untrained personnel in several key states. These data collection issues affect national food security interventions and advance estimates. Policymakers face challenges due to these significant gaps in agricultural statistics.

title: Hormuz crisis: Iran hit triggers fears of fuel supply disruption
content: An Iranian attack on a ship with Indian crew has heightened concerns. This incident raises worries about fuel and fertilizer supply disruptions. Indian authorities are closely monitoring the situation and seafarer advisories. The flare-up delays any plans for new ship movements from India. India has sufficient crude oil stocks and is monitoring global prices.

title: Delivery agent barges into woman's Bengaluru flat, flashes her
content: A Bengaluru food vlogger reported a frightening incident involving a delivery agent. The agent allegedly forced his way into her washroom and exposed himself. Police arrested the 22-year-old delivery agent, Vijay Mallikarjun Kamat, following the complaint. Kamat claimed he only needed to use the washroom and denied exposing himself. Flipkart confirmed the termination of the delivery partner's engagement after the incident.

title: WDMMA 2026 Global Rankings: IAF ranked 3rd air power for 5th year; stays ahead of China
content: The Indian Air Force is ranked third globally for the fifth consecutive time. Its fleet quality and balance surpass China's larger air force. Future acquisitions will significantly boost the Indian Air Force's fighter strength. New aircraft like Rafale and Tejas will enhance combat capabilities. India is also developing its fifth-generation Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft.

title: Small towns beat big cities in rooftop solar adoption
content: Smaller urban centers are leading India's rooftop solar adoption, surpassing major metropolitan areas. The Pradhan Mantri Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana promotes rooftop solar installations for households. This government scheme has seen rapid progress and aims to benefit many households. Over 36 lakh rooftop solar systems have already been installed across the country. Efforts are now focused on expanding urban reach and assisting low-income households.

title: Crypto Tax Refund In ITR 2026: Don't Miss These Steps To Claim Your Money
content: Crypto gains have to be disclosed under Schedule VDA while filing ITR-2 or ITR-3, depending on the taxpayer's profile.

title: Trump Remembers "True American Patriot" Who Called For 500% India Tariff
content: Graham used his position to push for steep tariffs on nations that imported Russian energy, including India, China, and Brazil. He introduced a bill proposing at least a 500 per cent tariff on nations purchasing Russian energy products.

title: Army Veteran, 92, Alleges Fraudulent Sale Of Allotted Land In Rajasthan
content: A 92-year-old retired officer alleges his Rajasthan land was fraudulently sold using forged documents.

title: Election Commission Mandates Parents' SIR Details For New Voters In Bihar
content: Election Commission mandates new voters submit parent SIR details with Form 6 for Bihar electoral rolls.

title: Ukrainian Drone Strike Sparks Fire At Industrial Zone In Russia
content: A Ukrainian drone strike ignited a fire in Russia's Stavropol industrial zone as Kyiv intensifies retaliatory attacks amid the ongoing war

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: US Launches Fresh Strikes On Iran As Fighting Flares Around Strait Of Hormuz
content: US-Iran War Live Updates: The flare-up is the latest to undermine an interim agreement between Washington and Tehran aimed at ending their war, which has caused global economic shockwaves since it began in late February.

title: India's 'Dark Data' Crisis: Why Experts Warn Of Million-Dollar Losses
content: Experts say companies will benefit more if they extract more value from existing information rather than endlessly collecting new data.

title: Vietnam Boat Tragedy Highlights: Vietnam Orders Probe After Boat Tragedy Kills 15 Indian Tourists
content: Vietnam Boat Tragedy Highlights: The speedboat was ferrying 32 Indian tourists, three crew members and one Vietnamese tour guide from May Rut Ngoai Islet to An Thoi International Port

title: 27 Killed, 22 Critical In Massive Fire At Bangkok Bar
content: People were seen rushing to escape as flames engulfed the entrance of the venue and thick smoke spread rapidly through the building.

title: US-Iran War Highlights: US Military Says Traffic "Flowing" In Strait Of Hormuz
content: Iran-US War Highlights: The US military has hit a variety of military sites and port facilities after blaming Iran for targeting several merchant vessels off the coast of Oman, sparking Iranian fire then as well.

title: Near-3,000-Year-Old Ramesside Period Tomb Discovered In Egypt's Luxor
content: The tomb, identified as belonging to a man named Paser, was found by a Dutch archaeological mission from Leiden University in the Sheikh Abd el-Qurna necropolis on Luxor's West Bank.

title: Over Union Minister's Satluj Stance, Punjab BJP Leader's 'Stay-In-Limit' Barb
content: Satluj was released on July 3 on the OTT platform ZEE5. Two days later, the platform took it down, triggering a massive controversy in Punjab.

title: Projectiles Hit Iran's Qeshm Island As Washington-Tehran Trade Blows
content: IRNA state news agency reported that "all of the targets were military" and that there had been no casualties in Iran

title: Israel Elections, To Determine Netanyahu's Fate, On October 27
content: The elections are being widely seen as a referendum on Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's leadership since the Gaza war erupted.

title: Iran's Fractured Regime Dooms Trump's Middle East Plan: John Bolton To NDTV
content: Former US National Security Adviser Ambassador John Bolton told NDTV the truce was never built to last because there is no single authority in Tehran capable of enforcing it

title: Premium Coffee, Colonial Cousins Vinyl: What PM Modi Gifted Australia's PM
content: One of the gifts, a Indian Premium Coffee Box, showcases the diversity of coffee grown across India's major coffee-producing regions.

title: France Temporarily Shuts Down 3 Nuclear Reactors Amid Heatwave
content: The measure is an environmental protection requirement to avoid discharging too much hot water into rivers already warming from the heatwave.

title: 9 Killed As Russia, Ukraine Intensify Attacks Against Each Other
content: The strikes came a day before Ukraine's allies were set to meet in Paris for talks on pressuring Russia to end its more than four-year war.

title: India Declares 1-Day Mourning For Ex-Qatar Emir Sheikh Hamad Al Thani
content: India declared a one-day national mourning on Monday as a mark of respect for former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al-Thani who died at the age of 74.

title: Bees inspired a smarter solar panel design millions of years after perfecting their hive
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. title: MEA condemns attack on tanker off Oman coast; 10 Indians rescued, 1 missing
content: attack on commercial vessel off the coast of Oman had 11 Indian nationals on board . the tanker was hit apparently by an Iranian strike for ignoring instructions to follow an approved route . Oman's maritime authority said it rescued 23 crew members but one was missing .
